# 'gs branch submit' sets the shared GS_OPERATION env contract for the
# message generator on both the create path (--fill) and the update
# path (--regenerate-message).

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-04-05T16:40:32Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

# set up a fake GitHub remote
shamhub init
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
shamhub register alice
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

# configure a generator that echoes the shared env vars
chmod 755 $WORK/gen.sh
git config spice.message.generator $WORK/gen.sh

# create and submit a branch with --fill (create path)
git add feature.txt
gs bc -m 'Add feature' feature1
gs branch submit --fill
stderr 'Created #'

shamhub dump changes
stdout 'op=branch-submit update=false branch=feature1 base=main'

# re-submit with --regenerate-message (update path)
cp $WORK/extra/feature-update.txt feature.txt
git add feature.txt
git commit -m 'update feature'

gs branch submit --regenerate-message
stderr 'Updated #'

shamhub dump changes
stdout 'op=branch-submit update=true branch=feature1 base=main'

-- repo/feature.txt --
Contents of feature
-- extra/feature-update.txt --
New contents of feature
-- gen.sh --
#!/bin/sh
op="${GS_OPERATION:-}"
up="${GS_MESSAGE_UPDATE:-}"
br="${GS_BRANCH:-}"
ba="${GS_BASE:-}"
printf '{"title":"op=%s update=%s branch=%s base=%s","body":"body"}' "$op" "$up" "$br" "$ba"
